output.dependency: The code for a specific quality measure (the short structured identifier) determines the detailed textual description of that exact measure, including the population/clinical criterion it evaluates.

A higher-level domain/category label for a measurement area determines the set of valid measure codes; the specific measure identifier is a subtype within that domain (e.g., surgical infection prevention measures).
The identifier for an organization/facility determines the corresponding facility name and the facility address/street line; all three fields refer to the same facility record and must align.
A locality/state set determines the postal code; the city (locality), state, and ZIP/area code are linked and must correspond to the same geographic location.

Within a given measurement area and measure description, the numeric patient count depends on which measure is being evaluated; the described patient population determines the reported number of patients for that measure.
The number of patients used as the denominator (or base population) determines the percent/result field’s value (a percentage summary derived from the count data for the same measure).
